other_observers: Cole Wild; found by Joey Kellner
description: the bird was a vireo just about the same size as a Warbling Vireo that was often in the same tree. The overall shape was similar to the Warbling Vireo, except the tail was relatively shorter, the head appeared a bit more rounded and the overall shape appeared a bit bulkier. The bill was short (relatively shorter than expected for a Warbling Vireo), and was black with a pale pinkish color at the base of the lower mandible. It was slightly but noticeably hooked, and thicker than expected for a warbler. The legs were relatively thick and short, and were blue-gray in color. The irides were dark.  The bird had a dark gray cap, contrasting with a white supercilium, which in turn contrasted with a black eye-line that extended from the base of the bill to the nape. There was a small area of white under the eye, larger than the eye-arc of many birds but not very extensive. The nape and back were dusky olive-green. The wings were grayish with some dusky olive-green highlights. The short tail was gray underneath.  The throat was a fairly bright dusky yellow color, brightest in the center and duller towards the sides and bottom of the throat. The belly and chest were lightly but noticeably tinged pale yellow; the flanks were also tinged this color, albeit a bit brighter than the belly. The undertail coverts were brighter than either the flanks or the belly, but not as bright as the throat.Behaviors: The bird was observed foraging in willows, from about 15 feet to 35 feet up.Call: none heardPlumage: unknown
photographer: Photographer: Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer
